stimulus: In 1968, Senegalese filmmaker Ousmane Sembène completed his second feature, Mandabi ______blank wry comedy about a Dakar laborer struggling to cash an unexpected money order, Sembène’s film was the first commercial release shot largely in Wolof rather than in French.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. A period correctly marks the boundary between one independent sentence ("In 1968…Mandabi") and another ("A wry comedy…French"). The phrase beginning with "A wry comedy" is an appositive modifying the subject of the next sentence, "Sembène's film."

Choice B is incorrect because it produces a comma splice between two independent sentences. Choice C is incorrect because inserting "which was a" turns the second sentence into a dependent clause attached by a comma, which still leaves the first sentence followed by an incomplete construction. Choice D is incorrect because it creates a run-on, fusing the sentences without punctuation.
